dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we have a problem. Someone's been talking.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found out about our arrangement and has rallied the guards against us. They've blockaded the road {location_hint} and seized half the {quest_item} you already gathered. If we don't act now, everything falls apart. I need you to find an alternate route to the stockpile and bring back twice as much: enough to outbid them and buy the guards' loyalty back. There are {enemy_type} on the back trail, but that's a problem you can handle.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're too deep to back out now. Either we win this or we lose everything.

playerResponses.text: Double the supplies, alternate route, hostile territory. Consider it done.
Your plan is unraveling fast. But I'll patch it together.
You've made too many enemies. I'm cutting my losses.

dialogueChunks.intro: Change of plans, {player_name}. A bigger opportunity just landed in my lap.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A merchant caravan heading through {location} lost their escort to {enemy_type} attacks {location_hint}. They're desperate for protection and they're carrying a fortune in {quest_item}. If you escort what's left of their cargo to my warehouse, I'll 'negotiate' a favorable storage fee. By the time they can arrange new transport, I'll have already brokered deals with their buyers. {target_npc} is trying to offer them shelter at the guild hall instead, so we need to move fast.
dialogueChunks.outro: Opportunity doesn't knock, {player_name}. It stumbles past wounded, and the clever ones are ready.

playerResponses.text: Ruthless, but effective. I'll bring the cargo.
Exploiting stranded merchants? You're something else. Fine.
Preying on desperate people crosses a line. I'm out.

dialogueChunks.intro: Bad news, {player_name}. Our competitor isn't just blocking us: they're trying to destroy us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} hired a group of sellswords to ransack my workshop and scatter my tools into the wilderness {location_hint}. Without those tools, I can't forge anything, and without product, my trade agreements collapse. I need you to recover my equipment and deal with whatever {enemy_type} have moved into the area since the sellswords stirred everything up. Oh, and bring back more {quest_item}: the furnace ate through the last batch faster than expected.
dialogueChunks.outro: They want a trade war? They'll get one. But first, I need my forge running again.

playerResponses.text: I'll get your tools back and clear the area.
Trade wars, sellswords, monsters: your ambition has expensive taste.
This feud is going to get someone killed. I want no part of it.
